request.question: Both Should Have Known Better and Carrie & Lowell have which instrumentalist playing on them?

title: The Broadside Tapes 1
Richard Nunns
I Should Have Known Better
U Should've Known Better
Ahmed Zuway
Scott Lucas (musician)
Pilipili
Should Have Known Better
Malcolm Laycock
Carrie & Lowell
sentences: The Broadside Tapes 1, alternatively known as "Broadside Ballads, Vol.
14", was a compilation of demo recordings done by Phil Ochs for "Broadside" magazine in the early-to-late 1960s.
Of the sixteen songs that appeared, ranging from the humorous ("The Ballad of Alferd Packer") to the depressing ("The Passing of My Life"), all were new to listeners.

It also included a song about the Profumo affair ("Christine Keeler") and it closed with a live cover of The Beatles' "I Should Have Known Better" (retitled "I Shoulda Known Better") featuring Eric Andersen on harmony vocals and harmonica.
Richard Nunns QSM (born 1945) is a Māori traditional instrumentalist of Pākehā heritage.
He is particularly known for playing the Taonga pūoro and his collaboration with fellow Māori instrumentalist Hirini Melbourne.
Since Melbourne's death, he is regarded as the world's foremost authority on Māori instruments.

"I Should Have Known Better" is a song by English rock band the Beatles composed by John Lennon (credited to Lennon–McCartney), and originally issued on "A Hard Day's Night", their soundtrack for the film of the same name released July 10, 1964.
"I Should Have Known Better" was issued as the B-side of the U.S. single "A Hard Day's Night" released July 13, 1964.
An orchestrated version of the song conducted by George Martin appears on the North American version of the album, "A Hard Day's Night Original Motion Picture Soundtrack."
"U Should've Known Better" is a song by American recording artist Monica.

It was written in collaboration with Harold Lilly and Jermaine Dupri, and produced by the latter along with frequent co-producer Bryan Michael Cox for her original third studio album, "All Eyez on Me" (2002).
When the album was shelved for release outside Japan, the song was one out of five original records that were transferred into its new version, "After the Storm" (2003).
A contemporary R&B slow jam, "U Should've Known Better" contains elements of soul music and rock music.
Built on an pulsating backbeat, the song's instrumentation consists of screeching guitars and an understated harp pattern.
Lyrically, Monica, as the protagonist, delivers a message of loyalty to her imprisoned love interest and sings about staying down for him despite his doubts.
Ahmed Mahmoud Zuway (Arabic: أحمد الزوي‎ ‎ ) (born December 28, 1982), better known better as Kabila, is a Libyan football striker.
He currently plays for Ahly Benghazi in the Libyan Premier League.
He is a member of the Libyan national football team.
He is known for his excellent heading ability and goal scoring.

David Scott Lucas (born May 10, 1970), known better as Scott Lucas, is an American musician, who is best known for being the guitarist, bassist, and lead vocalist for Local H, as well as the lead vocalist and guitarist of Scott Lucas and the Married Men.
Peter Gatonye (born November 1982), known better for his stage name Pilipili is a musician/singer from Kenya.
At the beginning of his career, he collaborated with another musician, Gun B.
Their best known song was "Nampenda".
He has also sung a song with Ratatat.
The song was called Morale.
"Should Have Known Better" is a song by American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Sufjan Stevens.
It is the second track and second single from his seventh studio album, "Carrie & Lowell", and was released digitally on March 11, 2015 on Asthmatic Kitty.
A promotional CD was later released on Asthmatic Kitty but was not available for sale.
Malcolm Richard Laycock (1 November 1938 – 8 November 2009) was a schoolteacher until moving into broadcasting, initially on Radio London.
He is now known better as a British radio presenter and producer, best known for his work on programmes related to jazz, dance band and big band music.
During his career he presented shows for both BBC Radio 2 and the BBC World Service.
Between 1995 and 2009, he was also the host of "Sunday Night at 10", a long running weekly Sunday evening show on Radio 2.
Carrie & Lowell is the seventh studio album by American musician Sufjan Stevens, released through Asthmatic Kitty on March 31, 2015.
Unlike Stevens's previous studio album, the electronic "The Age of Adz", "Carrie & Lowell" is sparsely instrumental and marks a return to the performer's indie folk roots.
The album was given a one-week preview on NPR.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Both Should Have Known Better and Carrie & Lowell have which instrumentalist playing on them?
hop1_docs: Should Have Known Better | "Should Have Known Better" is a song by American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Sufjan Stevens.  It is the second track and second single from his seventh studio album, "Carrie & Lowell", and was released digitally on March 11, 2015 on Asthmatic Kitty.  A promotional CD was later released on Asthmatic Kitty but was not available for sale.
I Should Have Known Better (disambiguation) | "I Should Have Known Better" is a song by The Beatles.

I Should Have Known Better | "I Should Have Known Better" is a song by English rock band the Beatles composed by John Lennon (credited to Lennon–McCartney), and originally issued on "A Hard Day's Night", their soundtrack for the film of the same name released July 10, 1964.  "I Should Have Known Better" was issued as the B-side of the U.S. single "A Hard Day's Night" released July 13, 1964.  An orchestrated version of the song conducted by George Martin appears on the North American version of the album, "A Hard Day's Night Original Motion Picture Soundtrack."

I Should Have Known Better (Jim Diamond song) | "I Should Have Known Better" was a UK number one single for one week in December 1984 for Jim Diamond.  The song was displaced after one week by Frankie Goes to Hollywood's song "The Power of Love".  Diamond publicly requested that people not buy his single, but instead buy "Do They Know It's Christmas? "

Homolidae | The family Homolidae, known as carrier crabs or porter crabs, contains 14 genera of marine crabs.  They mostly live on the continental slope and continental shelf, and are rarely encountered.  Members of the Homolidae have their fifth pereiopods (last pair of walking legs) in a sub-dorsal position, which allows them to hold objects in place over the rear half of the carapace.  The objects carried include sponges, black corals and gorgonians, and this behaviour may be a defence mechanism against predators.  Some species have been observed carrying living sea urchins in a symbiotic relationship which allows them to benefit from the protection of the urchin's dangerous spikes.

Armies in the American Civil War | This article is designed to give background into the organization and tactics of Civil War armies.  This brief survey is by no means exhaustive, but it should give enough material to have a better understanding of the capabilities of the forces that fought the American Civil War.  Understanding these capabilities should give insight into the reasoning behind the decisions made by commanders on both sides.

Jim Diamond (singer) | James Aaron Diamond (28 September 1951 – 8 October 2015) was a Scottish singer-songwriter, best known for his three Top 5 hits.  The first was "I Won't Let You Down" (1982), as the lead singer in the trio PhD, with Tony Hymas and Simon Phillips.  His solo performance, "I Should Have Known Better", was a United Kingdom No.1 in 1984.  The third track was the theme song from "Boon", "Hi Ho Silver" which reached No.5 in the UK Singles Chart in 1986.  He has also featured as a vocalist on the charity No.1s "You'll Never Walk Alone" with The Crowd and "Let It Be" with Ferry Aid.  His last UK chart success was with "Young Love (Carry Me Away)" in 1986.
hop1_titles: I Should Have Known Better (Jim Diamond song)
Armies in the American Civil War
response.summary_1: Passage evidence: '"Should Have Known Better" is a song by American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Sufjan Stevens' and the song "is the second track ... from his seventh studio album, 'Carrie & Lowell'," released digitally on March 11, 2015. Missing fact / bridge: which instrumentalist(s) are credited as performers on both the "Should Have Known Better" track and the Carrie & Lowell album (i.e., the personnel/liner credits). Retrieval direction: fetch the track- and album-level personnel/credits (album booklet, Wikipedia "Carrie & Lowell" personnel section, AllMusic/Discogs credits) to identify the overlapping instrumentalist(s).
response.hop2_query: Carrie & Lowell personnel "Should Have Known Better" credits liner notes AllMusic Discogs
hop2_docs: Should Have Known Better | "Should Have Known Better" is a song by American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Sufjan Stevens.  It is the second track and second single from his seventh studio album, "Carrie & Lowell", and was released digitally on March 11, 2015 on Asthmatic Kitty.  A promotional CD was later released on Asthmatic Kitty but was not available for sale.

I Should Have Known Better | "I Should Have Known Better" is a song by English rock band the Beatles composed by John Lennon (credited to Lennon–McCartney), and originally issued on "A Hard Day's Night", their soundtrack for the film of the same name released July 10, 1964.  "I Should Have Known Better" was issued as the B-side of the U.S. single "A Hard Day's Night" released July 13, 1964.  An orchestrated version of the song conducted by George Martin appears on the North American version of the album, "A Hard Day's Night Original Motion Picture Soundtrack."
I Should Have Known Better (disambiguation) | "I Should Have Known Better" is a song by The Beatles.

Four funnel liner | A four funnel liner, four funnelled liner or four stacker is an ocean liner with four funnels.  The SS "Great Eastern", launched on 31 January 1858 (a full 40 years ahead of any comparable ships), was the only ocean liner to sport five funnels.  As one funnel was later removed, the "Great Eastern", by default, became the first ocean liner to have four funnels.  However, after the Great Eastern became a four funnel ship she never carried passengers so really should not be considered the first four funnel liner.  The SS "Kaiser Wilhelm der Grosse", launched on 4 May 1897, was properly the first ocean liner to have four funnels and was one of the first of the golden era of ocean liners that became prominent in the early- to mid-20th century.  The most famous four funnel liners are the RMS "Titanic", which sank after striking an iceberg on her maiden voyage on 14 April 1912, and the RMS "Lusitania", which was torpedoed on 7 May 1915 during the First World War.

Stephen Thomas Erlewine | Stephen Thomas "Tom" Erlewine (born June 18, 1973, in Ann Arbor, Michigan) is an American music critic and senior editor for AllMusic.  He is the author of many artist biographies and record reviews for AllMusic, as well as a freelance writer, occasionally contributing liner notes.  He is the frontman and guitarist for the Ann Arbor-based band, Who Dat?
It Should've Been Me (album) | It Should've Been Me is the debut album of Zoot Money's Big Roll Band, released in 1965.  The album's liner notes were by Alexis Korner.

Dan Pickett (musician) | James Founty (August 31, 1907 – August 16, 1967), better known as Dan Pickett, was an American Piedmont blues and country blues singer, guitarist and songwriter.  He recorded fourteen tracks for Gotham Records in 1949, several of which have been issued more recently.  AllMusic noted that "Pickett had a distinctive rhythmic style and unique phrasing that makes his records compelling decades after his release".
response.summary_2: Both "Should Have Known Better" and the album Carrie & Lowell feature Sufjan Stevens as the performing multi-instrumentalist (the song is by Stevens and is a track on his Carrie & Lowell album).
